DISCOVERY 302, MAY-29-2024 HEARING ON PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO DEEM MATTERS ADMITTED. NOT ARGUED/CONTESTED. THE MATTER IS DISCUSSED. AT CONCLUSION OF DISCUSSION, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. NO OPPOSITION FILED. MOTION IS GRANTED. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER REPEATING VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND MUST E-MAIL IT TO THE JUDGE PRO TEM TO SCHWARTZ@CMSLAW.COM. Motion To Deem Matters Admitted
Off Calendar May-29-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: RE: PLAINTIFF TD BANK USA, N.A.'s Motion To Deem Matters Admitted.

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Jonathan Vaknin, Esq., specially appearing counsel for Plaintiff.
Isabella Mancini, in propria persona, Defendant.

Not argued/contested. The court notes that it did not receive notice of an intent to appear to oppose the court's tentative ruling. The matter is discussed. At conclusion of discussion, the court adopts its tentative ruling. No opposition filed. Motion is granted. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem to schwartz@cmslaw.com.

Judge Pro Tem: Andy Schwartz. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported. (302/JPT)
